value.subject: Siteimprove + Drupal Agent-to-Agent Integration: Internal Alignment
value.body:
Purpose: Bring the Drupal team up to speed on the Siteimprove agent-to-agent integration concept and begin scoping what a Drupal-specific connector would look like.

Background: EPAM and Siteimprove have been collaborating on an initiative to embed Siteimprove intelligence directly into CMS workflows via agent-to-agent patterns. The work so far has focused on Optimizely (which is now on hold), but the architectural thinking and use case definitions are (mostly) transferable. We are now pivoting to explore Drupal as the next CMS target, potentially along with Sitecore and Contentful. 

What we are trying to do (three use cases, sequenced by complexity):

The simplest entry point is a search intelligence agent that lives inside the content planning workflow. A user asks a natural language question about keyword strategy or topical authority, the agent calls Siteimprove's search intelligence APIs, and structured planning output comes back. No CMS writes, no publishing, read-only. This is the lowest-risk, highest-signal starting point.

The second use case is PDF accessibility checking integrated into content workflows. Siteimprove runs automated WCAG 2.2 AA checks against uploaded PDFs and returns structured feedback to the task or workflow that triggered the request. 

The third and most complex use case is agent-to-agent remediation (high priority), where Siteimprove identifies an accessibility or SEO issue and a CMS-side agent resolves it. This requires write access, content identity mapping (URL to content ID to component), workflow awareness, and security controls. It is not where we start, but it is where this is headed.

What we need from this call: A shared understanding of these use cases, an honest assessment of Drupal-specific feasibility (especially around the JSON API, content identity resolution, and workflow/publishing semantics), and alignment on a discovery approach. The prior work followed a rapid-prototyping model where investigation and scope definition happened in parallel, and we expect something similar here as part of a Discovery effort.

Attached for reference: Three documents covering the use case definitions (search intelligence, PDF accessibility, and agent-to-agent remediation) originally written for the Optimizely context, plus a video recording from today's session where the Siteimprove team showed us their initial work on a POC for connecting Drupal to Siteimprove.

value.subject: Ivoclar | CMS | Pimcore opportunity
value.body:
Hey Dustin!

Let me put here the initial summary from the client (Ivoclar).

 

Short Briefing for Potential Pimcore Partner

We are looking for a new partner to support and further develop our Pimcore landscape. The partner should provide:

  • Backend & frontend development
  • Maintenance & support ticket handling
  • Project execution
  • Project management
  • QA / testing support (preferred)

 

Current Landscape

Our Pimcore platform is hosted on-premise in Hamburg and managed by Metaways.
We operate a 4-system landscape:

  • S – Sandbox
  • D – Development
  • Q – Quality
  • P – Production (mirrored across 2 data centers for backup/resilience)

 

Scope within Pimcore

  • CMS
  • Academy (custom implementation, planned for removal in 2026)
  • eIFU (custom implementation)
    • Multitenant setup for multiple subbrands:
  • Milling Services (custom implementation)
  • PIM (custom implementation, currently not business-ready, future use under review)
  • IDS (custom event solution, optimized for tablets)

 

Integrations

The landscape includes integrations with:

  • ERP
  • Salesforce
  • CDC
  • Commerce
  • GoToWebinar
  • Azure AD
  • Celum
  • Akamai
  • Google Tag Manager
  • Fusedeck (planned replacement by Matomo in 2026)
  • Pardot / Formbuilder

 

Important Delivery Context

Some systems are validated / GxP-relevant, which means:

  • higher documentation standards
  • stricter implementation and release processes
  • more structured testing and change management

 

Upcoming Topics / Projects

  • Academy data archiving
  • Removal of Academy module
  • Promocodes
  • eIFU Sagemax Bioceramics
  • Pimcore 12 update

 

What We Expect from a Partner

We are looking for a partner who can:

  • take over an existing customized Pimcore setup
  • ensure stable support operations
  • deliver new projects and enhancements
  • provide clear technical documentation
  • work in a structured and reliable delivery model

value.subject: Fix Backgrounds of Adobe Sales Deck.
Last Mile: Weekly DEP Forecast Review
value.body:
Please find the Power BI report “NA Util with Forecast” here

Agenda
Utilization & Demand Forecasting (Next 3 Months) - 1 min
  • Demand Forecasting: Analysis of projected demand for the upcoming quarter.
DEP NA Candidate Availability (Next 3  Months) - 1 min
  • Resource Pipeline: Reviewing the availability of DEP NA candidates for the next 3 months.
Assignment Extensions - 5 mins
  • Extension Reviews: Discussion of all current assignments set to expire within the next 3 months.
Forecast Opportunity Updates - 23 mins
  • Pipeline Status: Review of tracked opportunities in the forecast
  • Probability & Timeline: Updates on "probability of win" and projected start dates
  • Action Items

Probability Descriptions
90%: Account is ready to assign candidates (if no assignments are made within a week, candidates from our side have been proposed and approved, we lower the probability).
If the start date is moved more than one week after assignment, we expect the creation of NB: Obligation Roles.
 
75%: Not competitive, verbal win, active MSA/SOW process, roles have been created in the staffing desk.
We are ready to propose candidates for the roles, interview, and book them on the project.
 
50%: Fast-moving deal, very high probability to win. Usually in the presale stage.
 
25%: Possibility. Just to put it on our radar. We have a preliminary staffing plan and this is either a large deal that we need to plan for in advance or it could start quickly.

  • Agentic Commerce

Would love your inputs as well. The goal is to have the following:

Offering - 
Definition
Client Value 
What to listen for
Delivery approach / timeline / cost
Where we’ve done it before (if we have)

value.subject: Brooks Sitecore Capabilities Prep
value.body:
Hi guys,

As discussed with Rob, I’m trying to lock dates for client presentation on the week of April 5th. We would like to present our resource availability and case studies.

Some notes:
  1. Brooks likes to have people onsite, physically in the office. Opportunity for us to relocate site core talent and start building capability on the West coast. We are already doing same for SalesForce.
  2. If we want to introduce India resources, we will have to explain our operating model. Direct West Coast to India coordination is challenging, so probably we will need a layer of senior talent in CEE.
  3. This meeting may become our introduction to new senior leader in Brooks org (confirming attendees). In this case we will need to add our “who is EPAM” drill.

Let’s meet and review what we have.

Thanks!

value.subject: Chanel - Sitecore Prep Call
value.body:
Adding sugary of the call with Sitecore team

Recap
Sitecore team members, along with Dustin from EPAM Systems USA, discussed Chanel North America's objectives for a personalization pilot project, focusing on leveraging Sitecore's decisioning engine and Content Hub for asset management. Key challenges identified include integrating existing systems, understanding Chanel's risk-averse approach to new technology like AI, and clarifying the need for a new Content Hub instance versus utilizing the existing one. The team aims to develop a solution for Chanel's digital team, prioritizing a "do no harm" approach to their current infrastructure and a rapid timeline for a holiday season launch.

Key points
  1. Meeting Purpose: Sitecore is providing EPAM with context on Chanel North America's personalization project to enable EPAM to propose solutions and address limitations.
  2. Chanel NA Project Goal: Chanel North America aims for a "personalization activation project" focused on skincare (with interest from fragrance/beauty) to personalize user experience based on behavior, leveraging existing data from Monetate and Salesforce.
  3. Pilot Timeline: The pilot is targeted to go live around the holidays, meaning it needs to be operational by October/November.
  4. Current Content Hub (Adbox) State: Chanel uses Content Hub globally ("Adbox") with a highly controlled environment, including a 12-14 month lag for new feature deployment and complex rights management for assets.
  5. AI Considerations: Chanel is risk-averse regarding AI for content generation; however, AI for asset findability and decisioning (not creative generation) might be considered, as their current Content Hub instance lacks AI features.
  6. Decisioning Engine: Chanel is interested in Sitecore's ability to aggregate data from multiple sources (Monetate, Salesforce) to power a robust decisioning engine for personalized content recommendations.
  7. Content Gap: Chanel's content audit revealed a significant subset of assets missing or inaccessible for personalization, leading to a perception that a new Content Hub instance might be needed.
  8. New Instance vs. Existing: Chanel NA believes they might need a new Content Hub instance for the pilot, but the global Sitecore team suggests leveraging and evolving the existing "Adbox" due to its foundation, compliance, and governance.
  9. Pilot Channels: The initial focus for the pilot is email, Product Detail Pages (PDP), and potentially the homepage.
  10. Next Steps for EPAM: EPAM will be introduced to Chanel's stakeholders (Rasha and VJ) to gather documentation and begin discussions on potential solution paths, with an understanding that the first meeting is for discovery, not presenting a full solution.

value.subject: Panel Discussion with EPAM clients: Lessons in Leadership, Life & Letting Go
value.body:
Panel Discussion: Lessons in Leadership, Life & Letting Go

Join us to this panel exploring the real meaning behind “having it all”. While this idea is often linked to taking on more roles and responsibilities, many find that true fulfillment comes from making intentional choices about where to invest time and energy.

In this panel discussion, our speakers will share personal stories of giving time, support, and opportunities—as well as moments when they chose to step back or change direction. They’ll reflect on how these decisions have shaped their leadership, personal priorities, and sense of purpose.

Held in the spirit of International Women’s Day and its “Give to Gain” theme, the discussion will focus on how contribution, boundaries, and a long-term perspective can shape meaningful careers and lives. This session offers a space for reflection for anyone navigating professional growth, leadership responsibilities, or changing personal goals.

Date & Time:

Wednesday,

March 04

9:30 – 10:30 a.m. (EST) 

 

Join Online

 

(For those who RSVP’d)

We invite you to get to know our distinguished speakers. The panel will be moderated by Sydney Goldstein, Lead Recruiter at EPAM.

Sarah Seager - Senior Director, Business Development 

Sarah is an accomplished professional with extensive experience in healthcare data science and analytics. Demonstrable technical leadership and innovation in various roles, contributing significantly to the effectiveness of geographical analysis in healthcare planning and the advancement of global real-world evidence analytics.

Elizabeth has over 20 years’ experience in Product Development and Information Technology. As Chief Information Officer of One Inc, she oversees the implementation of the company’s global IT roadmap, the development of overall strategic planning, our transformation and innovation initiatives, and the security of technology solutions in support of our insurance partners and customers.
 

Rebecca Short - Head of Office of the CIO

Rebecca is a senior operations and technology executive with extensive experience supporting large, global public companies through transformation, operational optimization, and strategic execution. She currently serves as Global IT Director for the Office of the CIO and Digital IT Operations at PPG, aligning technology investments with business strategy and strengthening governance, talent, and execution capabilities.
 

Kristie Vainikos Stegen - Chief Brand and Communications Officer

Kristie is overseeing all aspects of corporate marketing and communications for the company.

She is high-performing team uses an empathy-led approach to engage with internal and external audiences, building Cotality’s reputation and driving demand for its products and services.
 

Elena Parker - Chief Revenue Officer, Finance and Spend Management for Middle & Eastern Europe

value.body:
From: Jamila_Gramsey@epam.com
When: 2:30 PM - 3:00 PM March 4, 2026
Subject: EPAM DAM solution support exploration
Location: Microsoft Teams Meeting


Hi Jordan,

As promised, grabbing time to chat and learn more about your ask so that we can expeditiously provide a proper proposal for support.

Hi Team, 
See below for a summary of my chat with Jordan so you can have proper background context. I'm hoping to use this time to chat with him to codify resources and scope that will be suitable to get to the goal post. 

Talk soon,
Jamila

------------

Summary of Jordan’s Ask

Jordan is looking for a complete DAM (Digital Asset Management) solution that covers two major needs:

1. Core DAM Implementation & Integration

He wants a DAM that:

  • Manages and organizes assets correctly.
  • Includes strategic planning and a migration plan.
  • Connects effectively to the website.

2. Content Hub / Sales Enablement Layer

In addition to core asset management, he needs:

  • A content hub that supports sales enablement.
  • A structure for how sales teams will use and access content.

Resourcing Expectations

Jordan is unsure whether Adobe will include this work in their scope and if Adobe refuses I want us to be ready to step and and support the Mobility team. 

He’s asking you to help identify the right mix of roles:

Roles that may be needed:

  • A strategist/admin-level expert who can define “here’s how the DAM should work.”
  • A technologist / builder who can actually configure and build the system (a “keyboard warrior”).
  • Possibly nearshore/India resources for cost efficiency depending on the work.

From: Anton Kozak <Anton_Kozak@epam.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 17, 2026 12:37 AM
To: Molly Brennan <Molly_Brennan@epam.com>
Cc: John Capriotti <John_Capriotti@epam.com>; Oleg Vilchinski <Oleg_Vilchinski@epam.com>; Chris Wojtowicz <Chris_Wojtowicz@epam.com>; Ievgen Gritskov <Ievgen_Gritskov@epam.com>
Subject: CCC Marketing Portal modernization

 

Hi Molly,

 

John suggested requesting your support for a new opportunity that we discovered at Copyright Clearance Center (long-term $15M account).

Our CCC partners decided to start modernizing their Marketing website (currently built on WordPress, which has many issues, including accessibility).  

 

Ian Palmer (Head of Marketing) and Fran Alvarez (Enterprise Architecture, Spain) are leading this project and are considering a few options, including SF, HubSpot, and WordPress as a new platform. They are seeking our advice on choosing the best solution that meets a list of key capabilities for the Marketing team.

 

The main challenge is that CCC views us as a perfect engineering/delivery company, and we are not considered for other opportunities where advisory and expert-level expertise are King.   I want to break this perception so our team will be invited to large RFPs and really need support from DEP to get there.

 

Can we bring in a DEP SME to evaluate the data CCC has already collected (attached) and assist with evaluating enterprise content management / modern headless CMS solutions, so we can offer CCC an advisory workshop to help our partners better understand their options?

 

I estimate this project at approximately $400K, and I believe the advisory phase will open many new opportunities for us. It will help us earn points with different CCC business units (Marketing, Product, etc.) and gain a better perspective/support from Tracey Armstrong (CEO) and Babis Marmanis (Exec VP & CTO) for such deals.

 

Here is a link to CRM opportunity:

https://ms.crm.epam.com/main.aspx?etn=opportunity&pagetype=entityrecord&id=4B2E8618-CB02-F111-B4FD-000D3AAD7F91

 

Please let me know if a quick call would be helpful to clarify details and answer your questions.

 

Thank you,

Anton Kozak

VP, Account Management

TMP meeting
value.body:
Hi Dustin,

I’m setting up a series of meetings for TMP. Let me know if the time slot doesn’t work.


I refined a plan slightly.
  • Removed ASMT related stuff, since I'll handle that independently.

  • Added a column with specific items where I'll need your help.

  • Added priority column based on my vision.


I expect we will likely reshuffle and realign as we go. But that’s a starter.
Also, I’ll drop a couple of points I would like to discuss during our next meeting in the meeting chat by the end of this week.

Priority

Area

Purpose

Action Item

Where I need your help

1

AI Strategic Capability

Strengthen AI positioning beyond feature-level discussion

Strengthen AI-driven solution positioning by clearly articulating how AI enhances solution value.

Tangible results:

  • AI value articulation approach (business outcomes first)
  • AI-enabled reference architecture within Adobe ecosystem + extension points
  • Integration points with EL accelerators (co-relates with MarTech)
  •  GEO?
    • Define what I can help with - do or drive.
    • How to position so narrative ties to measurable business value especially on "what EPAM brings to the table".
    • How to articulate this so we don't overpromise and still are able to deliver - "AI value articulation approach (business outcomes first)"

2

MarTech Ecosystem Positioning

Expand beyond Adobe; articulate ecosystem integration clearly

Strengthen MarTech ecosystem positioning in Adobe-led opportunities by clearly articulating how Adobe integrates within broader stacks (CDP, CRM, MAP, Analytics).

Tangible results:

  • Ecosystem reference architecture diagram
  • Integration pattern summary/brief
  • Executive "Adobe in the Stack" positioning slides
  • Defined integration of EL accelerators
    • Help connecting with Alice and/or Ben to shape ecosystem vision.
    • Help forming strategic message before sharing with wider audience.

3

Strategic Positioning & Executive Communication

Improve executive framing and business-outcome focus

Refine proposal narratives to emphasize business outcomes over technical depth, explicitly linking architecture decisions to KPIs, risk mitigation, scalability, and revenue impact.

Tangible results:

  • KPI-to-architecture value mapping approach
  • Retrospective analysis of Jackson opportunity: 
    • What we could have positioned better
    • Where executive framing was weak 
    • Improvement actions
    • Conduct Jackson retrospective together.
    • Refine executive storytelling approach based on retro outcomes

4

Business Domain Fluency

Increase executive credibility and industry depth

Expand business domain fluency by conducting structured analysis in at least two key industries relevant to pipeline.

Tangible results (per industry):

  • Top business drivers & KPIs
  • Key challenges & regulatory pressures
    • Great suggesting to connect with Industry SMEs. That would be helpful

value.subject: Siteimprove A2A Connector
value.body:
Hey Vitali - We’ve seen some movement on the Siteimprove A2A opportunity and I wanted to get your input on approach but also potentially staffing.

Context:
Clay from Siteimprove wants to get moving on this in order to hit the July/July date he’s committed to his board. We met internally on Friday and tentatively aligned on a 4-week accelerated discovery effort. 

The objective is twofold:

  1. Fix and stabilize the existing non-AI Optimizely–Siteimprove connector (3 known defects).

  2. Design and scope a new Agent-to-Agent (A2A) connector leveraging Opal, targeting a Phase 1 delivery by end of June / early July.

This discovery phase will validate use cases, assess Opal capabilities and permissions, define the MVP boundary, and produce an SOW-ready Phase 1 implementation plan.

What I’d like to discuss with you is aligning on the right AI resource to support this 4-week effort, specifically:

  • AI/A2A architectural guidance around Opal

  • Support defining the A2A interaction model

  • Help ensure the Phase 1 plan is technically credible

We’re looking to staff this with a lean team (AI consultant + lead dev + BA) and are targeting an early March start.

value.subject: Avis + EPAM: Synthetic Audiences Demo
value.body:
Hi all!

 

EPAM would like to take this time to walk you through a demo of our AI accelerator tool: Synthetic Audiences

 

Agenda:

  • Intros
  • What are synthetic audiences?
  • What is their benefit and value to you?
  • Demo
  • Q&A

 

A little about the tool:

Synthetic Audiences helps marketing teams understand customer wants and needs faster. Part of the AI accelerator suite by Empathy Lab, it creates digital personas that behave like real people – to test content, concepts, and products at scale, delivering insights without the cost or delay of traditional research. 

 

Fully customizable and adaptable, you define persona attributes, choose segments to test, and deploy Synthetic Audiences in your own environment – keeping data secure and control in-house. Synthetic Audiences provides consistent, repeatable responses, with clear explanations tailored to what matters most for your team.

 

By connecting multiple data sources, it uncovers hidden patterns and deeper connections. Teams can iterate in minutes instead of weeks, reduce risk before launch, and make confident, data-driven decisions.

 

The result: research that’s faster, flexible, and cost-effective – combining human behavior with AI precision.

value.subject: A2A Integration Partnership: w/Siteimprove, Optimizely, and EPAM
value.body:
Agenda:

  • Connect as a broader team to align on the A2A partnership
  • Revisit the scope and the collective team’s support approach
  • Current Scope:

-          Build a true agent-to-agent integration between Siteimprove and Optimizely, enabling automated accessibility and digital governance remediation workflows, and moving beyond the limitations of the prior demo.

-          Support two major use cases: proactive remediation initiated within Optimizely (authors see and fix issues during content creation), and reactive remediation initiated in Siteimprove (audits and fixes applied to published content via Optimizely).

-          Ensure bidirectional interaction and workflow coverage, including approval workflows, authorization, and complex content mapping (URL-to-content ID), with attention to Optimizely’s diverse platform ecosystem (DXP, CMP, DAM, etc.).

-          Frame the integration as a reusable, strategic pattern for Siteimprove, aiming for minimal custom “glue” per platform and future applicability to other vendors, rather than a one-off connector.

-          Partner in promoting the engagement through demonstrations, documentation, joint white papers, and speaking events, etc.

value.body:
Apologies - moving due to some schedule conflicts.



All,

Owens Corning is currently undergoing a DXP selection exercise. We’d like to find a way to get introduced to the North America side of the business in order to be included in the implementation RFP when it comes out. 

We have been working with Owens Corning for 8 years and currently do some work on their Sitecore stack. 

This call is to align on who we know, could reach out to, etc. 

Thanks,
Dustin
